notes.content: disktest.sh now defaults to guided interactive mode on TTYs, supports --non-interactive for automation, includes interactive mode and disk selection, repeatable/comma-separated --device targeting, and writes per-disk reports under the run log directory's reports/ subdirectory.

fulltest.sh now emits explicit permission warnings before rebuild/reclone actions when build directories or cloned repos are root-owned/unwritable. The build directory startup preflight warns on existing unwritable ./build and clean/rebuild paths now explain ownership fixes.

test/gpu-fulltest-v2.sh is an experimental prepare-then-run variant of fulltest.sh. It keeps the stable fulltest.sh unchanged while separating detect, prepare-selected-tests, run-selected-tests, and summary phases.

disktest.sh interactive TTY flow now includes mode selection plus checkbox-style disk selection with arrow keys to move, space to toggle, enter to confirm, a to toggle all, and q to cancel. Default on TTY is guided interactive mode; use --non-interactive for automation.

fulltest.sh remains the stable production GPU validation path. test/gpu-fulltest-v2.sh is the experimental refactor lane for prepare-selected-tests-first behavior and should be validated on real GPU hosts before replacing fulltest.sh.

gpu-fulltest-v2.sh has fresh verification evidence for a mocked cuda-samples flow: prepare runs before execution, successful prep is reported in summary, and prep failures stop execution early with a prepare-failed summary.
As of 2026-04-10, repository is clean at main with latest human-readable decisions in docs/project-decisions.md and experimental GPU flow work isolated in test/gpu-fulltest-v2.sh.
Fixed disktest fio timeout/subshell bug causing 'FIO_BW_READ: unbound variable' under set -u by keeping run_fio execution in same shell scope and initializing fio metric vars. Commit 05c56ba pushed.

Improved disktest fio diagnostics: auto-select fio ioengine (io_uring/libaio/psync/sync), capture per-job stderr logs, and include error detail in WARN output for exit code 1. Added stderr artifacts to per-disk reports. Commit 563d9fd pushed.
Updated docs/disktest.md to clarify no auto-sudo behavior, raw-device permission safety checks, permission-denied fio symptom/remediation, and fio stderr artifact logs. Commit 31be1b5 pushed.

disktest interactive mode now warns non-root operators that quick/full/stress require sudo and suggests rerun command after mode selection; non-health safety checks enforce raw read/write permission before fio. Commit 99dd75f pushed.
Added non-interactive sudo warning path to disktest for quick/full/stress when not root, with rerun hint; documented behavior in docs/disktest.md. Commit 7986aaa pushed.

Improved PyTorch benchmark diagnostics in fulltest.sh and gpu-fulltest-v2.sh: torchrun output is mirrored to a temp transcript, failure summaries extract local_rank/exitcode clues, failed DDP repro scripts are kept in /tmp, and docs note the richer failure output. Commit 1ac4e63 pushed.

Added Python runtime warning to fulltest.sh and gpu-fulltest-v2.sh for PyTorch benchmark paths: logs active python3 and warns when Python 3.12+ is detected due to known torch.distributed/torchrun segfault history. Docs updated. Commit 28d6f45 pushed.

base-install.sh and amd-base-install.sh now install stress-ng, fio, lm-sensors, and uv; uv is installed via Astral's standalone installer to /usr/local/bin and removed during uninstall cleanup. Commit 9bfdebf pushed.

test/gpu-fulltest-v2.sh now prototypes a new node-stress mode that runs stress-ng CPU + RAM pressure together with the existing GPU stress backend for a user-defined duration in minutes (default 5). The stable fulltest.sh remains unchanged until the experimental lane is validated. Commit 9bfdebf pushed.

test/fulltest.sh now mirrors the validated node-stress mode from gpu-fulltest-v2.sh: concurrent stress-ng CPU + RAM load plus GPU burn, selectable with --node-stress-minutes (default 5), while preserving the existing GPU-only stress test. Commit pending at time of memory update.

Added standalone test/cpu-ram-stress.sh for CPU + RAM isolation using stress-ng only. It defaults to a 5-minute run, supports operator-tunable worker counts and reserve memory, auto-installs stress-ng when available and root is present, and is documented in docs/cpu-ram-stress.md and README.md.

base-install.sh now installs repo-root .aliases into the target user home as ~/.aliases, adds managed source blocks for ~/.bashrc and ~/.zshrc, and generates ~/.aliases.fish for fish with config.fish sourcing the generated file. Uninstall cleanup removes the managed alias blocks and fish wrapper file.
Added docs/cpu-test.md for test/cpu-test.sh, documenting the single-logical-CPU stress flow with socket0/socket1 selection, per-CPU runtime control, temperature logging, and resume/log files.

cpu-test.sh now writes each run into a per-run directory under /tmp by default, with optional --run-dir override for reusing the same transcript/progress state. The script still supports socket-aware single-CPU stress, resume markers, and optional temperature snapshots.
base-install.sh now also provisions the target user SSH authorized key and a passwordless sudoers drop-in when absent, in addition to copying repo aliases and installing uv/stress packages.
base-install.sh now adds the repo SSH authorized key for the target user, creates a passwordless sudoers drop-in when absent, and removes both managed access artifacts again during uninstall.

cpu-test.sh now writes stress_summary.txt in the per-run directory, updates it after each CPU, and flushes the run artifacts so a hang or power loss leaves a usable checkpoint alongside stress_test_log.txt and stress_progress.txt.

cpu-test.sh now operates at the CPU socket/package level instead of one logical thread at a time: each socket target runs stress-ng across all logical CPUs on that socket, defaults its run directory to /var/tmp for reboot-survivable checkpoints, supports --status to inspect saved state, and supports --reset-state to clear residue before retesting.

cpu-test.sh now supports both socket-level and per-thread targeting via --granularity socket|thread. Socket remains the default for package-level fault isolation, while thread restores the one-logical-CPU-at-a-time mode with the same run-dir, --status, and --reset-state crash/retest workflow.

cpu-test.sh now defaults its state directory to /var/tmp/cpu-test, supports --status without requiring --run-dir, supports --reset-status to clear summary/progress and exit without testing, and no longer stops after the first successful target because the pass/fail counters were changed to avoid set -e arithmetic-status exits.

cpu-test.sh now defaults to 300 seconds per target instead of 60 seconds, while retaining socket-level default targeting, optional per-thread granularity, stable /var/tmp/cpu-test status storage, and reset/status workflows.

Added install/install-raid.sh as an opt-in RAID / ESP redundancy installer. By default it stages the RAID helper scripts only; --activate is required to install and enable the apt hook and systemd timer/service, and non-RAID hosts remain unaffected unless the lane is explicitly activated. Documented the lane in docs/project-decisions.md, README.md, and install/raid/INSTALL.md.

cpu-test.sh default runtime is 300 seconds per target, docs/cpu-test.md now matches that default, and the help/status paths were re-verified. No active saved run state currently exists under /var/tmp/cpu-test.

directives.directive: Do not replace or heavily restructure test/fulltest.sh in place without real-host validation; use test/gpu-fulltest-v2.sh as the experimental lane for flow refactors first.
